agency: New York City Department of Cultural Affairs (DCLA)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — nyc.gov robots.txt only disallows /html/misc/). Fingerprinted nyc.gov/site/dcla (Akamai + nginx + NYC.gov 'Livesite' v22 platform + Dynatrace) and the DCLA Grants Management System at dclagms.nyc.gov/grants/s/ (identified as a Salesforce Experience Cloud community via the /grants/s/ redirect chain, LSKey-c$ cookies, and the culturalaffairsnyc.my.salesforce.com SAML issuer in the login markup). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Department of Cultural Affairs (DCLA)' via the Socrata Discovery API and pulled all 9 assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: NYC.gov 'Livesite' v22 info site (Akamai/nginx/Dynatrace) + a separate Salesforce Experience Cloud grants portal (dclagms.nyc.gov, backed by culturalaffairsnyc.my.salesforce.com); 9 Socrata datasets
meta.netnew: Apply for a Cultural Development Fund grant (GrantApplication) — the core transaction with no API, locked in the Salesforce Grants Management System
meta.tagline: DCLA publishes who it funds and for how much, but the grant application that produces those outcomes is locked in a Salesforce portal with no API.
meta.headline: The grantmaker pattern — funding outcomes are open across 9 datasets, but no owned API binds them and the grant application lives only in a login-walled Salesforce Experience Cloud portal.

scorecard_notes.platform: Shared NYC.gov Livesite CMS + a modern Salesforce Experience Cloud grants portal — maintained managed platforms, but not API-first.
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No content API; the grants portal is a login-gated Salesforce community with no documented public API.
scorecard_notes.openData: Good but thin — 9 datasets publish funding outcomes, the org directory, public art, and MFTA, but only the outcome side of the lifecycle.
scorecard_notes.coreData: Core entities present, but funding datasets key on organization name + application # with no stable org id, so joins are messy.
scorecard_notes.writeApi: No write API anywhere — applying for a grant goes only through the Salesforce Grants Management System.
scorecard_notes.agent: No agent-native surface today; no MCP, no OpenAPI, no schemas published by the agency.
scorecard_notes.ownership: Owns and maintains its open datasets, but the grant-application transaction runs on a vendor Salesforce org it configures rather than controls.

headline_findings: DCLA is a grantmaker that publishes outcomes but not the application: 9 NYC Open Data datasets cover program and capital grant awards, Cultural Institutions Group support, a geocoded cultural-organization directory, completed Percent for Art commissions, and Materials for the Arts donation summaries.

The core transaction — applying for a Cultural Development Fund grant — lives only in the Grants Management System (dclagms.nyc.gov), a Salesforce Experience Cloud community reached by login, with no public API and no Open Data twin.
No owned contract binds an organization to its funding; the funding datasets key on organization name and application # with no stable id, so an agent must stitch names across 9 Socrata IDs by hand.
Materials for the Arts data is published only in aggregate by year and category; no individual donor is ever exposed.
Net-new write surface: apply for a grant (GrantApplication) — the most important interaction between DCLA and its constituency, with no API today. See tech-stack.md and apis-observed.md.

apis_observed.note: 9 DCLA datasets; SODA /resource/<id>.json endpoints. The one real, open DCLA API — funding-outcome and directory data only.

register an org, create and submit a Cultural Development Fund grant application, track status. Salesforce Lightning Community (LSKey-c$ cookies, /grants/s/ path), backed by culturalaffairsnyc.my.salesforce.com via SAML SSO; no documented public JSON/OpenAPI.
content only; Akamai edge, nginx, Dynatrace RUM, mPulse. No content API.
Google Maps JS embedded on location pages.

fruit.name: Cultural Organizations directory
fruit.notes: The geocoded directory of cultural organizations DCLA funds and serves — discipline, address, phone, and full NYC geography spine (BIN, BBL, council district, NTA, lat/long).

fruit.name: Program Funding (grant awards)
opendata_match: DCLA Program Funding for FY11 rskq-5bfv (3c)
DCLA: Programs Funding for FY2010 j8p3-8ufc (3c)
fruit.notes: Cultural Development Fund grant AWARDS — organization, application #, and total final award per fiscal year. The outcome of the application; the application itself is not published.

fruit.name: Capital Funding
fruit.notes: Capital construction/renovation dollars by organization and fiscal year, broken out by source (DCLA, City Council, Borough President).

fruit.name: Cultural Institutions Group (CIG)
opendata_match: DCLA Cultural Institutions Group Funding ka27-qx5k (4c)
fruit.notes: Annual operating and energy support for the ~34 major institutions (museums, zoos, gardens, performing-arts centers) sited on City-owned property that DCLA funds directly.

fruit.name: Percent for Art public artworks
opendata_match: Completed Percent for Art projects with artist information gzdv-qiga (7c)
fruit.notes: Permanent public artworks commissioned under NYC's Percent for Art law — artist, sponsor/design agency, address, borough.

fruit.name: Materials for the Arts donations
opendata_match: DCLA Materials For The Arts - Donor Information vhtt-kpwy (6c)
fruit.notes: Creative-reuse donation summaries by year and category — donations, distinct donors, declared value, weight. AGGREGATE ONLY; no individual donor is published.

fruit.name: Apply for a grant (Cultural Development Fund)
fruit.notes: The single most important interaction between DCLA and its constituency — create, submit, and track a Cultural Development Fund grant application. No API and no Open Data twin; lives only in the Salesforce Experience Cloud Grants Management System (dclagms.nyc.gov). The net-new WRITE surface for this domain.
